mission: Freeze the Vecells target architecture as one authoritative ADR set plus one coherent architecture-view pack for the current baseline of phases 0-6, 8, and 9.

issues.title: Baseline architecture decisions existed only as scattered prior-task prose
issues.summary: Seq_016 freezes those decisions into one ADR set, decision matrix, and contract-binding pack.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 91 - The audit still treated route, settlement, release, and trust controls as phase-local conventions

issues.title: The Phase 0 control plane could still look phase-local
issues.summary: Seq_016 binds route intent, settlement, release freeze, trust, and readiness tuples into one cross-phase control-plane architecture decision.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 91 - The audit still treated route, settlement, release, and trust controls as phase-local conventions
forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 95 - The audit still omitted governance watch-tuple parity and recovery posture from release oversight

issues.title: Design-contract publication could drift outside runtime publication
issues.summary: Seq_016 freezes design-contract publication inside the runtime publication and release parity tuple.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 118 - Token export and design-contract conformance could still drift outside the published runtime tuple

issues.title: Operations and governance continuity and resilience authority were separate mental models
issues.summary: Seq_016 unifies watch tuples, readiness, trust, continuity proof, and recovery posture under one architecture control plane.

issues.title: Assistive capability could appear mandatory or architecturally central
issues.summary: Seq_016 freezes assistive functionality as optional sidecar capability with kill-switch posture.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 17 - AI assistance was modeled as a mandatory linear stage

issues.title: Tenant and acting-scope drift risk remained open
issues.summary: Seq_016 freezes exact ActingScopeTuple law for privileged and cross-organisation actions.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 114 - Tenant and acting context could still drift between governance scope and live cross-organisation work

issues.title: Artifact preview and handoff mode truth could remain implementation detail
issues.summary: Seq_016 treats artifact mode truth as a runtime publication and disclosure contract.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 115 - Artifact preview and handoff still lacked one live mode-truth contract for constrained channels

issues.title: Patient degraded mode, continuity proof, and shell continuity could become route-local
issues.summary: Seq_016 freezes continuity proof, same-shell law, and degraded posture as cross-phase architecture.

issues.title: Phase 7 embedded NHS App channel remains outside the current accepted baseline
issues.summary: The deferred ADR keeps compatibility seams only and blocks accidental re-entry into the current baseline.

issues.title: HSM-backed signing keys are still a provisioning seam
issues.summary: Seq_015 intentionally stopped at the release-control seam. The architecture freeze inherits that gap without changing the baseline.

issues.title: Concrete on-call destinations still need tenant and service-owner binding
issues.summary: Seq_016 keeps alert routing architecture fixed while leaving final rotation identifiers to provisioning work.
